results.evidence: delete/route.ts:47-48 calls updateSearchShard(searchKv, entry, 'remove') before db.$transaction at line 57. Test: delete/__tests__/route.test.ts:135-143 and 145-153 verify call order.

delete/route.ts:34 uses { id: true, ...SEARCH_ENTRY_SELECT } in findFirst select, expanding from the old 4-field select to all 17 SEARCH_ENTRY_SELECT fields. Test: delete/__tests__/route.test.ts:166-187 asserts all key fields present.
delete/route.ts:49 calls invalidateSearchIndex(searchKv) after updateSearchShard. Test: delete/__tests__/route.test.ts:155-164 verifies ordering via callOrder array.

delete/route.ts:42-54 wraps all KV ops in try/catch with console.error logging. DB $transaction at line 57 runs unconditionally regardless of KV outcome. Tests: delete/__tests__/route.test.ts:195-204 (DB still runs on KV failure), 206-212 (kvCleanup: false), 214-226 (error logged).

search/route.ts:169-178 validates edge-only results via db.skill.findMany({ where: { name: { in: names } }, select: { name: true } }). Builds Set and filters. Test: search/__tests__/route.test.ts 'filters stale edge results against DB' confirms stale-deleted is excluded.

search/route.ts:169 only enters validation when searchSource === 'edge'. For 'edge+postgres' and 'postgres', validation is skipped. Tests: search/__tests__/route.test.ts 'skips validation for edge+postgres source' and 'skips validation for postgres source' both assert mockSkillFindMany not called.

search/route.ts:179-181 catch block logs console.warn with descriptive message and returns unfiltered results. Test: search/__tests__/route.test.ts 'returns all edge results unfiltered when DB validation fails' verifies 200 status, all 5 results, and warning logged.

search-index.ts:276 passes { expirationTtl: SHARD_TTL_SECONDS } (604800) to kv.put in the batch write loop, which handles both name shards and author shards (allWrites array at lines 268-273 collects both). Tests: search-index.test.ts 'buildSearchIndex TTL' suite verifies name shards, author shards each get TTL.
search-index.ts:321 (name shard puts) and :343 (author shard put) both pass { expirationTtl: SHARD_TTL_SECONDS }. Tests: search-index.test.ts 'updateSearchShard TTL' suite verifies both.
search-index.ts:286 (buildSearchIndex meta) and :356 (updateSearchShard meta) both pass { expirationTtl: SHARD_TTL_SECONDS }. Tests verify meta key puts include TTL.

findings.title: invalidateSearchIndex fallback kv.put missing expirationTtl
findings.description: The invalidateSearchIndex() function has a fallback path (line 372) when kv.delete is unavailable. It writes an empty meta object via kv.put(META_KEY, ...) but does NOT include { expirationTtl: SHARD_TTL_SECONDS }. Every other kv.put in this file uses the TTL. This fallback meta entry would persist indefinitely instead of self-expiring after 7 days, contradicting the defense-in-depth TTL strategy. While this path is only hit when kv.delete is unavailable (rare), it is an inconsistency that undermines AC-US3-03's intent.
findings.suggestion: Add { expirationTtl: SHARD_TTL_SECONDS } to the fallback kv.put call in invalidateSearchIndex: kv.put(META_KEY, JSON.stringify({...}), { expirationTtl: SHARD_TTL_SECONDS })

findings.title: Existence validation fires when Postgres was deliberately skipped (pgSkipped=true)
findings.description: When edge returns 200+ results (edgeResults.length >= fetchLimit), the route sets pgSkipped=true and skips Postgres entirely for performance. However, pgResults stays empty, so searchSource is set to 'edge' (line 164-165). This triggers the existence validation block (line 169) which calls getDb() and runs a findMany with up to 200 names against Postgres. This partially negates the optimization of skipping Postgres. In the pgSkipped case, the system is operating normally (edge has a full set), so stale entries are unlikely and the extra DB round-trip is wasteful. The spec says validation only runs for edge-only results (AC-US2-01 says 'Postgres returned zero results'), but pgSkipped is a different scenario from 'Postgres returned zero' -- it means Postgres was never called.
findings.suggestion: Guard the existence validation with an additional check: if (searchSource === 'edge' && !pgSkipped && results.length > 0). This preserves the optimization while still validating when Postgres genuinely returned nothing.
